test.name: Mixed-site curated list: host-filter to Ancestry and dedupe repeated URLs
test.description: external_links_search returns a flat mixed-site, duplicate-heavy list (the same Ancestry probate URL three times under different labels, plus MyHeritage / Find A Grave / FamilySearch-wiki / Newspapers.com entries). The skill's consumption rules: filter by URL host to the requested site, dedupe, and pick the most specific collection match. The probate search for Thomas Flynn (Patrick's likely father) is a real research thread in this scenario.

input.user_message: Search Ancestry for Thomas Flynn's probate or will records in Schuylkill County, 1870 to 1890.

judge_context: Should use only ancestry.com URLs from the curated list — the MyHeritage, Find A Grave, FamilySearch-wiki, and Newspapers.com entries are not Ancestry options and should not be presented as such
The same Ancestry probate collection URL (collections/8800) appears three times under different labels — it should be collapsed to one option, not presented as three different collections

Should pick the probate/wills collection (matching the plan's record type) as the search base, not the 1850 census collection, and append the search parameters to the curated URL rather than rebuilding a site-wide URL from scratch
A research-log entry must be written in the same turn the URL is generated: site ancestry, outcome partial, capture_received false

This test grades host-filtering, deduping, and collection match — NOT date-encoding style. The 1870–1890 window may be encoded as a representative year (e.g. a midpoint plus a note) or as an explicit range; both are acceptable, so do not penalize using a single year (e.g. death=1880) instead of a range as an unsupported claim or over-narrowing.
